devanagari: कपिल उवाच । अथ यो गृहमेधीयान् धर्मानेवावसन्गृहे । काममर्थं च धर्मान् स्वान् दोग्धि भूयः पिपर्ति तान् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: kapila uvācaatha yo gṛhamedhīyān dharmānevāvasangṛhekāmamarthaṃ ca dharmān svān dogdhi bhūyaḥ piparti tān
translation:
Kapila said:
Now, a person who sticks to domestic life and performs the (religious) duties prescribed for householders, obtains from them the two objectives, viz. kāma (enjoyment of desired objects) and artha (wealth). He continues to perform the same duties again.

devanagari: स चापि भगवद्धर्मात् काममूढः पराङ्मुखः । यजते क्रतुभिर्देवान् पितॄंश्च श्रद्धयान्वितः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sa cāpi bhagavaddharmāt kāmamūḍhaḥ parāṅmukhaḥyajate kratubhirdevān pitṝṃśca śraddhayānvitaḥ
translation: He also is so much deluded with the objects of enjoyments, that he becomes averse to the Bhāgavata dharma. Endowed with earnest faith, he continues to worship the gods and Pitṛs (ancestors) by performing sacrifices.

devanagari: तत् श्रद्धया क्रान्तमतिः पितृदेवव्रतः पुमान् । गत्वा चान्द्रमसं लोकं सोमपाः पुनरेष्यति ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tat śraddhayā krāntamatiḥ pitṛdevavrataḥ pumāngatvā cāndramasaṃ lokaṃ somapāḥ punareṣyati
translation: The man has his mind completely possessed of faith (in gods and Pitṛs). He observes the religious vows (for the propitiation) of manes and gods, and drinks Soma juice (in the Soma sacrifice). Such a man will attain to the heaven presided over by the Moon, (but) will come back (i.e. will be born) again to this world.

devanagari: यदा चाहीन्द्रशय्यायां शेतेऽनन्तासनो हरिः । तदा लोका लयं यान्ति ते एते गृहमेधिनाम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā cāhīndraśayyāyāṃ śete'nantāsano hariḥtadā lokā layaṃ yānti te ete gṛhamedhinām
translation: (But) when Hari who is seated on Ananta (Śeṣa) goes to sleep on the bed of that Lord of serpents (at the time of Pralaya at the end of Brahmā’s day) those regions (accessible to such householders) are (also) dissolved.

devanagari: ये स्वधर्मान्न दुह्यन्ति धीराः कामार्थहेतवे । निःसङ्गा न्यस्तकर्माणः प्रशान्ताः शुद्धचेतसः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ye svadharmānna duhyanti dhīrāḥ kāmārthahetaveniḥsaṅgā nyastakarmāṇaḥ praśāntāḥ śuddhacetasaḥ
translation: The wise persons who do not perform their religious duties for obtaining kāma (their desired objects) and artha (wealth), who are unattached and have deposited (offered) all their religious acts (in God as his worship); who are extremely serene and of pure mind; who are engaged in the Nivṛtti- dharma; who have given up the sense of ‘mine-ness’ (ownership) and I-ness (ahaṃkāra)—such wise persons, by their power called ‘observance of one’s duties’ (Svadharma), and by thoroughly purified mind, go through the portals of the Sun to the perfect (or omniscient) Puruṣa (the Supreme Man), who is the Lord of the universe (of the movables and immovables, the liberated and the unliberated etc.), and who is the material cause of the world, and who causes the creation and the destruction of the universe.

devanagari: निवृत्तिधर्मनिरता निर्ममा निरहङ्कृताः । स्वधर्माप्तेन सत्त्वेन परिशुद्धेन चेतसा ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nivṛttidharmaniratā nirmamā nirahaṅkṛtāḥsvadharmāptena sattvena pariśuddhena cetasā
translation: The wise persons who do not perform their religious duties for obtaining kāma (their desired objects) and artha (wealth), who are unattached and have deposited (offered) all their religious acts (in God as his worship); who are extremely serene and of pure mind; who are engaged in the Nivṛtti- dharma; who have given up the sense of ‘mine-ness’ (ownership) and I-ness (ahaṃkāra)—such wise persons, by their power called ‘observance of one’s duties’ (Svadharma), and by thoroughly purified mind, go through the portals of the Sun to the perfect (or omniscient) Puruṣa (the Supreme Man), who is the Lord of the universe (of the movables and immovables, the liberated and the unliberated etc.), and who is the material cause of the world, and who causes the creation and the destruction of the universe.

devanagari: सूर्यद्वारेण ते यान्ति पुरुषं विश्वतोमुखम् । परावरेशं प्रकृतिं अस्योत्पत्त्यन्तभावनम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sūryadvāreṇa te yānti puruṣaṃ viśvatomukhamparāvareśaṃ prakṛtiṃ asyotpattyantabhāvanam
translation: The wise persons who do not perform their religious duties for obtaining kāma (their desired objects) and artha (wealth), who are unattached and have deposited (offered) all their religious acts (in God as his worship); who are extremely serene and of pure mind; who are engaged in the Nivṛtti- dharma; who have given up the sense of ‘mine-ness’ (ownership) and I-ness (ahaṃkāra)—such wise persons, by their power called ‘observance of one’s duties’ (Svadharma), and by thoroughly purified mind, go through the portals of the Sun to the perfect (or omniscient) Puruṣa (the Supreme Man), who is the Lord of the universe (of the movables and immovables, the liberated and the unliberated etc.), and who is the material cause of the world, and who causes the creation and the destruction of the universe.

devanagari: द्विपरार्धावसाने यः प्रलयो ब्रह्मणस्तु ते । तावद् अध्यासते लोकं परस्य परचिन्तकाः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: dviparārdhāvasāne yaḥ pralayo brahmaṇastu tetāvad adhyāsate lokaṃ parasya paracintakāḥ
translation: Those who meditate upon Hiraṇyagarbha (Brahmā) as the Supreme Being[1], stay in the Satyaloka (Brahmā’s region) to the end of the second Parārdha which is the time of god Brahmā’s Pralaya (the mahāpralaya indicating the end of Brahmā’s period).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
parasya paracintakāḥ: Yogins who meditate upon the Paramātman—Bhāgavata Candrikā
VC. notes that those who meditate upon Hiraṇyagarbha only are not liberated after Brahmā’s liberation.

devanagari: क्ष्माम्भोऽनलानिलवियन् मनैन्द्रियार्थ । भूतादिभिः परिवृतं प्रतिसञ्जिहीर्षुः । अव्याकृतं विशति यर्हि गुणत्रयात्मा । कालं पराख्यमनुभूय परः...
english_devnagari: kṣmāmbho'nalānilaviyan manaindriyārthabhūtādibhiḥ parivṛtaṃ pratisañjihīrṣuḥavyākṛtaṃ viśati yarhi guṇatrayātmākālaṃ parākhyamanubhūya paraḥ svayambhūḥ
translation: [2] When the great god Brahmā enjoys his full span of life called Parārdha, he desires to withdraw the universe composed of the gross elements, viz., the earth, water, fire, wind and the sky, the mind, the sense-organs along with their objects and the ahaṃkāra. He becomes one with the Prakṛti composed of three guṇas and enters the unmanifest Brahman.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī gives a different process of this saṃhāra or withdrawal: Brahmā is withdrawn into the unmanifest Lakṣmī along with the deities presiding over all Tattvas. He enters Parabrahman through Lakṣmī.

devanagari: एवं परेत्य भगवन्तमनुप्रविष्टा । ये योगिनो जितमरुन्मनसो विरागाः । तेनैव साकममृतं पुरुषं पुराणं । ब्रह्म प्रधानमुपयान्ति अगताभिमानाः ॥ १०...
english_devnagari: evaṃ paretya bhagavantamanupraviṣṭāye yogino jitamarunmanaso virāgāḥtenaiva sākamamṛtaṃ puruṣaṃ purāṇaṃbrahma pradhānamupayānti agatābhimānāḥ
translation: The Yogins who have controlled their breath and mind and are unattached to worldly objects reach along with Brahmā (Hiraṇyagarbha) to the immortal highest Brahman, the ancient Puruṣa; (for till then) they have not yet shed off their ego (aharhkāra) completely.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
agatābhimānāḥ: They are proud of being the votaries of Hiraṇyagarbha. Hence they are not completely absorbed in the Supreme Lord. Their dissolution being prākṛtic in nature, they are born again—VC.

devanagari: अथ तं सर्वभूतानां हृत्पद्मेषु कृतालयम् । श्रुतानुभावं शरणं व्रज भावेन भामिनि ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: atha taṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ hṛtpadmeṣu kṛtālayamśrutānubhāvaṃ śaraṇaṃ vraja bhāvena bhāmini
translation: Oh brilliant mother, you devoutly take shelter under him who is enshrined in the lotuslike hearts of all beings and whose glory you have heard (from me).

devanagari: आद्यः स्थिरचराणां यो वेदगर्भः सहर्षिभिः । योगेश्वरैः कुमाराद्यैः सिद्धैर्योगप्रवर्तकैः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: ādyaḥ sthiracarāṇāṃ yo vedagarbhaḥ saharṣibhiḥyogeśvaraiḥ kumārādyaiḥ siddhairyogapravartakaiḥ
translation: (Even god Brahmā is born again). God Brahmā (who bears the Vedas within him) is the first (i.e. the creator) of the movable and immovable world. Along with sages (like Marīci), great Yogins like Sanatkumāra etc., and Siddhas who have propagated yoga path, even he—

devanagari: भेददृष्ट्याभिमानेन निःसङ्गेनापि कर्मणा । कर्तृत्वात्सगुणं ब्रह्म पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhedadṛṣṭyābhimānena niḥsaṅgenāpi karmaṇākartṛtvātsaguṇaṃ brahma puruṣaṃ puruṣarṣabham
translation: [4] Having attained to the Saguṇa Brahman, the foremost among the Puruṣas on account of his actions done without any desire for their fruit, but on account of his notion of being different[5] (from god) as a creator and the (consequent) ahaṃkāra about creation,
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Padaratnāvalī explains: Jīva attains to the Lord (Puruṣa) by proper understanding of the exact differences between Jīva, īśvara and by complete knowledge that is so essential for Liberation (mukti) and by doing actions without any desire even after attainment of knowledge. The Lord is Brahman, i.e. full of all excellences. He possesses infinite guṇas like knowledge, power etc. and is Saguṇa as the creator of the universe. He assumes Human form for his devotees. Hence he is called Puruṣa. He is beyond kṣara and akṣara Puruṣa. Hence he is called Puruṣarṣabha. Brahmā ‘enters’ into him, that is, gets sāyujya type liberation.

Footnote [5]:
bhedadṛṣṭi: (i) Jīva’s ego as being independent and the wrong identification of the body and the Soul—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) The ego of being the creator of the world just as Viṣṇu is the protector—VC.
Even Sanatkumāra and others had the egoistic tinge of being the experiencers of Brahman and they regarded Brahman as ‘spotted’ with Māyā—VC.

devanagari: स संसृत्य पुनः काले कालेनेश्वरमूर्तिना । जाते गुणव्यतिकरे यथापूर्वं प्रजायते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa saṃsṛtya punaḥ kāle kāleneśvaramūrtinājāte guṇavyatikare yathāpūrvaṃ prajāyate
translation: [He] is born again as before at the time of the (next) creation when the balance of three guṇas gets disturbed and the guṇas get into commotion by the force of Time (Kāla) which is a form of the Lord.

devanagari: ऐश्वर्यं पारमेष्ठ्यं च तेऽपि धर्मविनिर्मितम् । निषेव्य पुनरायान्ति गुणव्यतिकरे सति ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: aiśvaryaṃ pārameṣṭhyaṃ ca te'pi dharmavinirmitamniṣevya punarāyānti guṇavyatikare sati
translation: They (the sages etc.) also, having enjoyed the divine glories and positions accrued to them by their religious acts, are born again when the universe is created (lit. guṇas get mixed up at the time of creation).

devanagari: ये त्विहासक्तमनसः कर्मसु श्रद्धयान्विताः । कुर्वन्ति अप्रतिषिद्धानि नित्यान्यपि च कृत्स्नशः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ye tvihāsaktamanasaḥ karmasu śraddhayānvitāḥkurvanti apratiṣiddhāni nityānyapi ca kṛtsnaśaḥ
translation: Those whose minds are attached here to Karmas, perform with faith all the daily religious duties as well as those (kāmya) actions which are not prohibited by the Dharma Śāstra.

devanagari: रजसा कुण्ठमनसः कामात्मानोऽजितेन्द्रियाः । पितॄन् यजन्ति अनुदिनं गृहेष्वभिरताशयाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: rajasā kuṇṭhamanasaḥ kāmātmāno'jitendriyāḥpitṝn yajanti anudinaṃ gṛheṣvabhiratāśayāḥ
translation: Those whose mind has become dull by rajo-guṇa, and is attached to enjoyments, have no control over senses. Their heart finds pleasure in domestic life. These (persons) propitiate the Pitṛs (ancestors).

devanagari: त्रैवर्गिकास्ते पुरुषा विमुखा हरिमेधसः । कथायां कथनीयोरुविक्रमस्य मधुद्विषः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: traivargikāste puruṣā vimukhā harimedhasaḥkathāyāṃ kathanīyoruvikramasya madhudviṣaḥ
translation: Those who value only the first three objectives in life (viz. dharma, artha and kāma) set their face against the stories of Hari (the vanquisher of demon Madhu) whose great prowess is worth eulogizing and memory about whom eliminates the saṃsāra.

devanagari: नूनं दैवेन विहता ये चाच्युतकथासुधाम् । हित्वा शृण्वन्ति असद्‍गाथाः पुरीषमिव विड्भुजः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ daivena vihatā ye cācyutakathāsudhāmhitvā śṛṇvanti asad‍gāthāḥ purīṣamiva viḍbhujaḥ
translation: They are certainly of accursed fate who leaving aside the nectarlike stories of Hari, listen to the vile, accounts just as feces-eating animals feed upon excrement.

devanagari: दक्षिणेन पथार्यम्णः पितृलोकं व्रजन्ति ते । प्रजामनु प्रजायन्ते श्मशानान्तक्रियाकृतः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: dakṣiṇena pathāryamṇaḥ pitṛlokaṃ vrajanti teprajāmanu prajāyante śmaśānāntakriyākṛtaḥ
translation: They go to the region of Pitṛs (ancestors) through the southern path of Aryaman (technically called dhūmra-mārga—path of smoke). Those who perform all the prescribed religious rites from the pregnancy—garbhādhāna—to the funeral, are born in their own family (lit. of their descendants).

devanagari: ततस्ते क्षीणसुकृताः पुनर्लोकमिमं सति । पतन्ति विवशा देवैः सद्यो विभ्रंशितोदयाः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tataste kṣīṇasukṛtāḥ punarlokamimaṃ satipatanti vivaśā devaiḥ sadyo vibhraṃśitodayāḥ
translation: Oh pious mother, thereafter when their merit (accrued to them by their religious acts) is exhausted, they are immediately deprived of their means of (celestial) enjoyments by Gods. They being helpless (at the mercy of their karmas) fall again to this world.[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Cf. kṣīṇe puṇye martya-lokaṃ viśanti / Bhagavad Gītā 9.21.

devanagari: तस्मात्त्वं सर्वभावेन भजस्व परमेष्ठिनम् । तद्‍गुणाश्रयया भक्त्या भजनीयपदाम्बुजम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāttvaṃ sarvabhāvena bhajasva parameṣṭhinamtad‍guṇāśrayayā bhaktyā bhajanīyapadāmbujam
translation: Therefore you adore the Supreme Lord (Viṣṇu) with utmost regard and devotion based on (i.e. felt on account of contemplation of) his excellent attributes. The lotus-like feet of the Lord deserve service.

devanagari: वासुदेवे भगवति भक्तियोगः प्रयोजितः । जनयत्याशु वैराग्यं ज्ञानं यद्‍ब्रह्मदर्शनम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsudeve bhagavati bhaktiyogaḥ prayojitaḥjanayatyāśu vairāgyaṃ jñānaṃ yad‍brahmadarśanam
translation: If the yoga called devotion to Lord Vāsudeva is intensely practised, it immediately generates desirelessness and knowledge that leads to the realization of Brahman.

devanagari: यदास्य चित्तमर्थेषु समेष्विन्द्रियवृत्तिभिः । न विगृह्णाति वैषम्यं प्रियं अप्रियमित्युत ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: yadāsya cittamartheṣu sameṣvindriyavṛttibhiḥna vigṛhṇāti vaiṣamyaṃ priyaṃ apriyamityuta
translation: (As a matter of fact) all objects are equal. But it is when the mind of the devotee becomes fixed and steady in God due to the votary’s love for the excellent attributes of the Lord that it does not discriminate (between them) according to the attitude of the senses—as being favourite and agreeable and non-favourite and disagreeable.

devanagari: स तदैवात्मनात्मानं निःसङ्गं समदर्शनम् । हेयोपादेयरहितं आरूढं पदमीक्षते ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tadaivātmanātmānaṃ niḥsaṅgaṃ samadarśanamheyopādeyarahitaṃ ārūḍhaṃ padamīkṣate
translation: At that time (in that stage) he realizes the Brahman by his own Self as being free from all attachment, of perfect wisdom,[7] free from acceptability or rejectability (i.e. above merits and demerits) and full of the highest bliss.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
sama-darśana—(i) One who gives pure knowledge—Siddhāntapradīpa
(ii) One who knows the reality as it is—Padaratnāvalī
(iii) One who knows all world as imbued with Brahman—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: ज्ञानमात्रं परं ब्रह्म परमात्मेश्वरः पुमान् । दृश्यादिभिः पृथग्भावैः भगवान् एक ईयते ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānamātraṃ paraṃ brahma paramātmeśvaraḥ pumāndṛśyādibhiḥ pṛthagbhāvaiḥ bhagavān eka īyate
translation: The Para Brahman is pure knowledge (consciousness). It is described as the Supreme Ātman, the Īśvara and the Puruṣa. The Lord (Bhagavān) is the same who is equally perceived in different capacities (as the seer, the thing-to-be- seen and the act-of-seeing).

devanagari: एतावानेव योगेन समग्रेणेह योगिनः । युज्यतेऽभिमतो ह्यर्थो यद् असङ्गस्तु कृत्स्नशः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvāneva yogena samagreṇeha yoginaḥyujyate'bhimato hyartho yad asaṅgastu kṛtsnaśaḥ
translation: Perfect non-attachment (to the world) in all respects is the only desired fruit that a Yogin is to get by practising all yogas in this world.

devanagari: ज्ञानमेकं पराचीनैः इन्द्रियैर्ब्रह्म निर्गुणम् । अवभात्यर्थरूपेण भ्रान्त्या शब्दादिधर्मिणा ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānamekaṃ parācīnaiḥ indriyairbrahma nirguṇamavabhātyartharūpeṇa bhrāntyā śabdādidharmiṇā
translation: [8] The Brahman is one (without a second). It is of the nature of knowledge or consciousness and without any attributes. It is an illusion when through outward looking senseorgans it appears as things (like the sky) possessed of sound and other attributes.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
(i) Bhāgavata Candrikā Quoting Bhagavad Gītā 14.27 interprets ‘Brahman’ as jīva. Jīva is of the nature of knowledge, destitute of guṇas like sattva. To regard it as identical with its gross body is illusion. Jīva is conditioned by elements like Pṛthvī but the Supreme Soul is beyond these.
(ii) Brahman which incarnated as a Fish, should be understood as possessing non-prākṛtic body. It appears as one endowed with a gross body to the senses which are familiar with gross objects. But that is a delusion.—Padaratnāvalī
(iii) The Brahman which is of the nature of knowledge appears to outward-looking sense-organs as objects (like the sky) possessing the attribute of sound. It is devoid of rejectable attributes.—Siddhāntapradīpa

devanagari: यथा महान् अहंरूपः त्रिवृत् पञ्चविधः स्वराट् । एकादशविधस्तस्य वपुरण्डं जगद्यतः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā mahān ahaṃrūpaḥ trivṛt pañcavidhaḥ svarāṭekādaśavidhastasya vapuraṇḍaṃ jagadyataḥ
translation: Just as the one Mahat (Mahattattva) appears as ahaṃkāra of three types (viz. sāttvika, rājasa and tāmasa) and of five kinds (according to the five Mahābhūtas) and eleven kinds (as per ten sense-organs plus the internal organ, viz. the mind), it is from the same principle that the Svarāj (jīva), its body and its egg (of the universe) make their appearance.

devanagari: एतद्वै श्रद्धया भक्त्या योगाभ्यासेन नित्यशः । समाहितात्मा निःसङ्गो विरक्त्या परिपश्यति ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: etadvai śraddhayā bhaktyā yogābhyāsena nityaśaḥsamāhitātmā niḥsaṅgo viraktyā paripaśyati
translation: Verily it is only a non-attached person whose mind is composed and serene by faith, devotion and continuous practice of Yoga who realizes this Brahman.

devanagari: इत्येतत्कथितं गुर्वि ज्ञानं तद्‍ब्रह्मदर्शनम् । येन अनुबुद्ध्यते तत्त्वं प्रकृतेः पुरुषस्य च ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ityetatkathitaṃ gurvi jñānaṃ tad‍brahmadarśanamyena anubuddhyate tattvaṃ prakṛteḥ puruṣasya ca
translation: Oh mother, I have up till now explained to you the knowledge that leads to the realization of the Brahman. It is by this knowledge that the real nature of Prakṛti and Puruṣa is clearly understood.

devanagari: ज्ञानयोगश्च मन्निष्ठो नैर्गुण्यो भक्तिलक्षणः । द्वयोरप्येक एवार्थो भगवत् शब्दलक्षणः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānayogaśca manniṣṭho nairguṇyo bhaktilakṣaṇaḥdvayorapyeka evārtho bhagavat śabdalakṣaṇaḥ
translation: The path of knowledge (Jñāna yoga) pertains to the attributeless Brahman while the Yoga called Bhakti (devotion) is based on firm devotion to me. But both of them have the same objective viz. (the realization of) the Supreme Lord.

devanagari: यथेन्द्रियैः पृथग्द्वारैः अर्थो बहुगुणाश्रयः । एको नानेयते तद्वद् भगवान् शास्त्रवर्त्मभिः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yathendriyaiḥ pṛthagdvāraiḥ artho bahuguṇāśrayaḥeko nāneyate tadvad bhagavān śāstravartmabhiḥ
translation: Just as the same object possessing many attributes is perceived in different forms by the sense-organs with separate functions (lit. doors), (similarly) the Supreme Lord (though absolutely one without a second) is seen in different ways through different śāstras.

devanagari: क्रियया क्रतुभिर्दानैः तपःस्वाध्यायमर्शनैः । आत्मेन्द्रियजयेनापि सन्न्यासेन च कर्मणाम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kriyayā kratubhirdānaiḥ tapaḥsvādhyāyamarśanaiḥātmendriyajayenāpi sannyāsena ca karmaṇām
translation: By doing religious acts, by performing sacrifices, by donating gifts, by penance, by the study of the Vedas, by subduing the ātman and the sense-organs and by renunciation of karmas.

devanagari: योगेन विविधाङ्गेन भक्तियोगेन चैव हि । धर्मेणोभयचिह्नेन यः प्रवृत्तिनिवृत्तिमान् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yogena vividhāṅgena bhaktiyogena caiva hidharmeṇobhayacihnena yaḥ pravṛttinivṛttimān
translation: By means of the yoga with (eight) different stages, and by the Path of Bhakti (Bhaktiyoga), by religious practices both with and without the desire for their fruits, which are called Pravṛtti and Nivṛtti.

devanagari: आत्मतत्त्वावबोधेन वैराग्येण दृढेन च । ईयते भगवानेभिः सगुणो निर्गुणः स्वदृक् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmatattvāvabodhena vairāgyeṇa dṛḍhena caīyate bhagavānebhiḥ saguṇo nirguṇaḥ svadṛk
translation: By clear knowledge about the nature of the Soul and by firm sense of non-attachment—by means of these, the self-illuminating Lord whether Saguṇa or Nirguṇa is realized.

devanagari: प्रावोचं भक्तियोगस्य स्वरूपं ते चतुर्विधम् । कालस्य चाव्यक्तगतेः योऽन्तर्धावति जन्तुषु ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: prāvocaṃ bhaktiyogasya svarūpaṃ te caturvidhamkālasya cāvyaktagateḥ yo'ntardhāvati jantuṣu
translation: I have clearly described to you the nature of the four kinds of Bhakti-yoga and of kāla (Time) whose course is unmanifested but which runs within the beings (to bring about their birth and death).

devanagari: जीवस्य संसृतीर्बह्वीः अविद्याकर्म निर्मिताः । यास्वङ्ग प्रविशन्नात्मा न वेद गतिमात्मनः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: jīvasya saṃsṛtīrbahvīḥ avidyākarma nirmitāḥyāsvaṅga praviśannātmā na veda gatimātmanaḥ
translation: (I have narrated to you) the external courses of jīva, which are created by avidyā and karma. Oh mother, when the Soul enters into these, he does not know its own real nature.

devanagari: नैतत्खलायोपदिशेन् नाविनीताय कर्हिचित् । न स्तब्धाय न भिन्नाय नैव धर्मध्वजाय च ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: naitatkhalāyopadiśen nāvinītāya karhicitna stabdhāya na bhinnāya naiva dharmadhvajāya ca
translation: This knowledge should not be explained to the evil person nor to one of undisciplined (arrogant) nature, nor to a dullard nor to a man of bad character, nor to a hypocrite.

devanagari: न लोलुपायोपदिशेत् न गृहारूढचेतसे । नाभक्ताय च मे जातु न मद्‍भक्तद्विषामपि ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: na lolupāyopadiśet na gṛhārūḍhacetasenābhaktāya ca me jātu na mad‍bhaktadviṣāmapi
translation: (One should not) advise this to a person of greedy nature, nor to a person whose mind is attached to his house (property etc.), nor to one who is not devoted to me. It should never be taught to the enemies of my devotees.

devanagari: श्रद्दधानाय भक्ताय विनीताय अनसूयवे । भूतेषु कृतमैत्राय शुश्रूषाभिरताय च ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: śraddadhānāya bhaktāya vinītāya anasūyavebhūteṣu kṛtamaitrāya śuśrūṣābhiratāya ca
translation: It should be taught to my faithful devotee, who is modest and disciplined and is not jealous of anybody; to one who has formed friendship with beings, and who takes pleasure in serving (his elderly persons or preceptor).

devanagari: बहिर्जातविरागाय शान्तचित्ताय दीयताम् । निर्मत्सराय शुचये यस्याहं प्रेयसां प्रियः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: bahirjātavirāgāya śāntacittāya dīyatāmnirmatsarāya śucaye yasyāhaṃ preyasāṃ priyaḥ
translation: It should be expounded to him who is completely unattached internally and externally, who is of tranquil heart, and is not envious of anybody, is pure and to whom I am the dearest of the dear.

devanagari: य इदं श्रृणुयाद् अम्ब श्रद्धया पुरुषः सकृत् । यो वाभिधत्ते मच्चित्तः स ह्येति पदवीं च मे ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: ya idaṃ śrṛṇuyād amba śraddhayā puruṣaḥ sakṛtyo vābhidhatte maccittaḥ sa hyeti padavīṃ ca me
translation:
Oh mother, the man who even once hears this knowledge with faith, or relates this to others with his mind set on me verily attains to my abode.
[back to top]
